articles.title: How to Watch The Knicks Championship Parade Live Online
articles.why: The Knicks championship parade is a live cultural event happening tomorrow — the Canyon of Heroes, 53 years in the making, with Alicia Keys performing 'Empire State of Mind.' This is New York's moment, and the parade is the exclamation point. Essential viewing logistics for anyone tuning in.
articles.hook: The Knicks' first championship parade since 1973 rolls through the Canyon of Heroes tomorrow morning, with Alicia Keys performing 'Empire State of Mind' at the finish.

articles.title: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
articles.why: Tim Cook publicly saying Apple price increases are 'unavoidable' due to a memory shortage is a rare admission that directly affects every Apple customer — prices are going up across iPhones, Macs, and iPads, and the CEO is saying so plainly.
articles.hook: Tim Cook just told the Wall Street Journal that Apple price increases are unavoidable — a global memory shortage is forcing the company's hand.

articles.title: This ‘Benson & Brunson’ Knicks Tee Celebrates Jalen Brunson and Mariska Hargitay’s Endearing Friendship
articles.why: The Knicks championship is the city's biggest cultural moment in over 50 years, and this tee — merging Jalen Brunson with Mariska Hargitay's Olivia Benson — captures the exact texture of New York's collective delirium. It's covered by 8 sources, meaning the crossover has genuine viral traction.
articles.hook: Someone made a Jalen Brunson x Olivia Benson T-shirt and it might be the most New York object ever created.

articles.title: ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Trailer Gives First Look At Mark Ruffalo’s Hulk
articles.why: Spider-Man: Brand New Day is one of the most anticipated superhero films of the summer, and a new trailer dropping with the first look at Hulk entering the story is a genuine franchise moment that will dominate pop culture conversation today.
articles.hook: The new Spider-Man trailer just revealed Mark Ruffalo's Hulk, and Peter Parker is losing control of his powers.

articles.title: Jalen Brunson, Josh Hart to Throw Out First Pitch at Yankees Stadium Tonight: Stream the Baseball Game Online Free
articles.why: Jalen Brunson and Josh Hart throwing out the first pitch at Yankee Stadium the night of the Knicks parade is New York's championship week reaching its full cultural peak — the city treating its basketball heroes like royalty across every major institution.
articles.hook: Brunson and Hart are throwing out the first pitch at Yankee Stadium tonight as New York's championship week hits full delirium.

articles.title: ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Has a Brand New Trailer, and Spidey Is Freaking Out
articles.why: Spider-Man: Brand New Day is one of the most anticipated superhero films of the summer, and a new trailer dropping with the first look at Hulk entering the story is a genuine franchise moment that will dominate pop culture conversation today.
articles.hook: The new Spider-Man trailer just revealed Mark Ruffalo's Hulk, and Peter Parker is losing control of his powers.

articles.title: OG Anunoby Tells Alicia Keys ‘The City’s Asking for You’ Ahead of Knicks Ceremony Performance
articles.why: Alicia Keys performing 'Empire State of Mind' at the Knicks championship ceremony is the right artist, right song, right moment — a genuine New York cultural full-circle that anchors the city's biggest sporting celebration in decades.
articles.hook: OG Anunoby personally called Alicia Keys to tell her the city needed her at the Knicks championship ceremony.

articles.title: Google Suggests 'Captain Clutch Brunson' When Fans Search Jalen Brunson
articles.why: Google autofilling 'Captain Clutch Brunson' is a small but vivid marker of how completely the Knicks championship has landed in the cultural conversation — the internet is canonizing Brunson in real time.
articles.hook: Google is now autofilling 'Captain Clutch Brunson' when fans search his name — the internet has given him his title.

articles.title: Stephon Marbury Has a Message for Victor Wembanyama Before Knicks Parade
articles.why: Stephon Marbury — the original tragic Knick — demanding a public apology from Wembanyama over the Brunson shove on the eve of the championship parade is the kind of New York drama that perfectly captures this moment's energy.
articles.hook: Stephon Marbury is demanding Wembanyama publicly apologize for shoving Jalen Brunson before he'll let New York enjoy the parade in peace.

articles.title: What Color Is the Reflecting Pool? An Investigation.
articles.why: Workers pouring hydrogen peroxide into the National Mall's Reflecting Pool to turn it the 'right shade of blue' ahead of July 4 is a vivid, absurd image that captures something real about the current administration's relationship with spectacle and symbolism.
articles.hook: Workers spent yesterday morning pouring hydrogen peroxide into the Reflecting Pool trying to turn it the shade of blue Trump prefers — it's currently green with algae.

articles.title: Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
articles.why: The Atlantic's photo essay on World Cup fan passion is doing something the data stories can't — it puts a human face on the global convergence happening on US soil right now, and it's striking photography.
articles.hook: The Atlantic's photo essay on World Cup fan passion captures the scale of what's happening across North America right now better than any match recap.

articles.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
articles.why: Roelof Botha joining SpaceX's board just days after the largest IPO in history is a meaningful governance signal — Sequoia's former leader bringing institutional credibility to a company now navigating public market pressures for the first time.
articles.hook: Days after the biggest IPO ever, SpaceX just added one of Silicon Valley's most powerful investors to its board.

articles.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
articles.why: Snap's stock sliding on the day of its most ambitious hardware launch captures a real tension: the market isn't convinced that $2,195 AR glasses are a viable consumer product, even as the company bets its future on wearables.
articles.hook: Snap unveiled its most ambitious product ever — AR glasses at $2,195 — and the market responded by selling the stock.

articles.title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
articles.why: A Russian-speaking group allegedly compromising tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls — with credentials exposed for Oracle, Chevron, FedEx, and a NATO contractor — is a serious, ongoing cybersecurity event with real infrastructure stakes.
articles.hook: Russian-speaking hackers allegedly broke into Fortinet firewalls used by Oracle, Chevron, FedEx, and a NATO defense contractor — tens of thousands of networks exposed.

articles.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
articles.why: Only 16% of Americans believing AI will have a positive societal impact — while nearly half use it regularly — captures the defining contradiction of the AI moment: mass adoption paired with deep institutional distrust.
articles.hook: Nearly half of Americans use AI chatbots regularly, but only 16% think AI will have a positive impact on society.

articles.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
articles.why: Google's first new smart speaker in six years — rebuilt around Gemini rather than the command-driven Google Assistant — is a concrete signal of how the AI transition is reshaping consumer hardware categories that had gone stagnant.
articles.hook: Google just released its first new smart speaker in six years, and it's built around Gemini instead of the old Google Assistant.

articles.title: Tom Dreesen, Stand-Up Comedian Who Opened for Frank Sinatra, Dies at 86
articles.why: Tom Dreesen was one of the last living links to the Rat Pack era — the only comedian Frank Sinatra kept as a touring opener for 13 years. His death closes a chapter in American comedy that ran from the civil rights era through the late-night golden age.
articles.hook: Tom Dreesen, the only comedian Frank Sinatra kept as his personal opener for 13 straight years, has died at 86.

articles.title: Tom Dreesen Dies: Comedian And Favorite Of Carson, Letterman & Sinatra Was 86
articles.why: Tom Dreesen was one of the last comedians who existed at the intersection of classic American showbiz — Frank Sinatra's opening act, a Carson regular — and his death closes a chapter on a specific era of stand-up that no longer exists.
articles.hook: Tom Dreesen, the comedian who was Frank Sinatra's go-to opening act and a Johnny Carson staple for decades, has died at 86.

articles.title: NBA Trade Rumors: Jaylen Brown To Trail Blazers, Spurs, Or Jazz?
articles.why: The Jaylen Brown trade rumor is the opening move in what will be the NBA's defining offseason story — Boston potentially breaking up a Finals runner-up roster to chase Giannis has real implications for multiple franchises.
articles.hook: The Celtics are apparently ready to trade Jaylen Brown, and the Trail Blazers, Spurs, and Jazz are all being mentioned as destinations.

articles.title: CVS Is Switching to Aluminum Pill Bottles
articles.why: CVS switching its entire pill bottle supply chain from plastic to aluminum is a quiet but meaningful sustainability move from one of the largest pharmacy chains in America — the kind of infrastructure-level shift that rarely gets attention but affects millions of people.
articles.hook: CVS is ditching plastic pill bottles entirely and switching to aluminum — a small change that affects the roughly 4 billion prescriptions filled in the US every year.

articles.title: An Ascendant Constitutional Theory Is a Threat to American Science
articles.why: The unitary executive theory being applied to scientific funding — potentially allowing political appointees to override peer review — is a slow-moving but significant threat to how American science actually functions.
articles.hook: A constitutional theory gaining traction in Washington could give the executive branch direct control over which scientific research gets funded — overriding peer review entirely.

articles.title: The Toy Story Franchise Forgot Its Own Message
articles.why: With Toy Story 5 tracking for a record Pixar opening, The Atlantic's critical argument that the franchise has forgotten its own message about letting go is a sharp cultural counterweight to the hype.
articles.hook: The Atlantic argues that Toy Story 5 — about letting go and moving on — is itself the thing that refuses to let go.

articles.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
articles.why: A veteran VC who called Facebook's rise early arguing that the real AI winners won't be the companies selling AI — but those embedding it invisibly into other things — is a counterintuitive investment thesis worth understanding right now.
articles.hook: The VC who predicted Facebook's dominance says the real AI winners won't be selling AI at all — they'll be the ones you never notice are using it.

articles.title: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
articles.why: Epic enabling Fortnite skins to travel across games via Unreal Engine 6 is the most concrete step yet toward the interoperable metaverse that the industry has been theorizing about for years — a real product move, not just a vision.
articles.hook: Epic is making it so your Fortnite skins can follow you into other games — the interoperable metaverse is finally a real product plan.

articles.title: We got free GTA V upgrades before GTA VI
articles.why: Rockstar offering free GTA V upgrades to current-gen just months before GTA VI launches is a smart piece of timing — it re-engages a massive player base and signals the transition window is closing.
articles.hook: Rockstar is giving away free GTA V upgrades to current-gen consoles — months before GTA VI drops.

articles.title: SanDisk’s new PlayStation 5 SSD will cost you more than three PS5 Pros
articles.why: An 8TB PS5 SSD priced near $3,000 — more than three PS5 Pros — is a sharp illustration of how premium storage pricing has completely decoupled from the hardware it serves, and it's generating genuine consumer bewilderment.
articles.hook: SanDisk just released an 8TB PS5 SSD that costs nearly $3,000 — more than three PS5 Pros combined.

articles.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
articles.why: Amazon and QuEra promising useful quantum error correction by 2028 — ahead of previous timelines — is a credible near-term milestone from serious players, and moves quantum computing from perpetual 'five years away' territory into something more concrete.
articles.hook: Amazon and QuEra are promising useful quantum error correction by 2028 — earlier than almost anyone expected.
